title: lycopodiella cernua var. sikkimensis
intro: Lycopodiella cernua var. sikkimensis, also known as the Sikkim club moss, is a type of plant that belongs to the club moss family (Lycopodiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Lycopodiella cernua var. sikkimensis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, Lycopodiella cernua var. sikkimensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
